content.text:
Now I can clearly see all the covalent bonds connected to **N:3**. From this zoomed and rotated view, atom **N:3** has three covalent bonds going to:

1. **C:2** — to the upper-left (the carbon connected to H:41, H:42)
2. **C:4** — to the upper-right (part of the five-membered ring)
3. **C:7** — to the lower side (also part of the five-membered ring)

## Summary

**Atom of interest:** N:3 (Nitrogen)

**Neighbour atoms (covalently bonded):**

| Atomic Index | Element |
|:---:|:---:|
| 2 | C |
| 4 | C |
| 7 | C |

**geomconf_index:** 9146

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 3, whose element should be N. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
